ch.r: Self-employed land-bound physical work, weather-dependent. AI tools (Plantix, satellite crop monitoring) augment decisions but cannot substitute the labour. Mechanisation is uneconomic at India's 1.1 ha avg holding size.
Outdoor manual wage labour. AI exposure essentially zero — capital cost of automation far exceeds labour cost at Indian wage levels.
Animal handling, milking, fodder management — predominantly physical. IoT sensors and breed-management apps assist but the core labour is hands-on.
Outdoor physical work; AI used in fish-stock prediction and aquaculture monitoring, but field labour is irreducible.
Manual outdoor work on heterogeneous sites. Robotics adoption is decades away in Indian construction at current wage levels.
Project design and management; AI assists with CAD, structural analysis, and BIM. Significant productivity uplift but the on-site coordination work remains human.
Drafting, rendering, and concept exploration are now substantially AI-augmented (Midjourney, Stable Diffusion architectural workflows, AutoCAD generative design). Junior drafting work most exposed.
Operating textile machinery and finishing work. Computer-vision QC is emerging, but core fabric handling is dexterity-bound and India's cheap labour delays automation.
Skilled machine operation with significant procedural element. AI-vision quality inspection and predictive maintenance reshape parts of the work; the operator role remains.
Mixed assembly + skilled machining. Indian auto-component industry adopting robotics + AI inspection, but slower than global peers due to wage arbitrage.
Visual inspection is the canonical computer-vision task. Major Indian manufacturers (Tata, Mahindra, Bajaj) are deploying AI vision systems; 1.2M jobs are structurally exposed.
Process optimisation, simulation modelling, and quality systems design. AI assists but cross-functional coordination and on-shop-floor judgement remain human.
In-person retail interaction is hard to automate, but back-end inventory and demand-forecasting tasks are increasingly AI-driven, reducing total store staffing.
B2B trade relationships, credit decisions, and logistics coordination. AI agents emerging in supplier discovery and pricing, but the trust-based wholesale model holds.
UPI ubiquity + self-checkout = direct substitution. Reliance Retail, DMart, Amazon Fresh India are all piloting cashier-less formats. 4.5M jobs structurally exposed within 5 years.
Customer service is the most directly substituted task category by current LLMs. Indian D2C brands routinely report 60-80% of tier-1 support handled by AI agents.

ch.src: TCS Q4 FY25 + Jul 2025 layoff announcement
ch.r: Mid-tier offshore code/test/maintain work is the exact task profile AI coding assistants do best. The July 2025 12,000-layoff is the loudest signal yet. Senior architects less exposed.
Same task profile as TCS. Parekh's Jevons-paradox bet (10× productivity → 10× more software → more engineers) is plausible but unverified. Mid-tier engineers most exposed.
Mid-tier offshore application services. Net headcount declined for two consecutive years (FY24 -24K, FY25 -708). Structural exposure is high.
Similar task profile to top-3. CEO Vijayakumar explicitly: 'clients want to double revenue with half the headcount.' Linearity between revenue and headcount is breaking.
Telecom-heavy IT services with significant network-operations work. Network-ops AI agents are maturing; core engineering work follows the same trajectory as TCS/Infosys.
LTIMindtree, Coforge, Mphasis, Persistent. Same business model as top-5 but less pricing power. More exposed because clients consolidate vendors as productivity rises.
Tier-2/3 firms compete on price for commoditised work. AI coding tools have eliminated the wage arbitrage that justified their existence. Highest displacement risk in IT services.
Engineering at Indian unicorns and product startups. AI is a productivity multiplier; firms are hiring AI-native engineers at the senior end, slowing junior hiring. Net effect mixed.
Indian freelance developers on Upwork/Toptal compete globally on price. AI coding tools compress that arbitrage; commodity freelance work most exposed.
In-house engineering at MNCs in India. AI is a productivity tool; the work is closer to product engineering than IT services. Less commoditised, more defensible.
Engineering R&D for global parents (chips, automotive, industrial). AI assists simulation, design exploration, and code generation. Core domain expertise remains scarce.
Data engineering, BI, and analytics. AI is augmentative — AI-native data tooling (Hex, Snowflake Cortex, Databricks AI) makes each analyst 2-3× more productive but demand is rising faster.
Captive shared-services work (finance ops, HR ops, supply-chain coordination). Same task profile as BPM — high direct exposure to AI agents and document AI.

ch.src: Zinnov 2025 - 120K+ AI pros in Indian GCCs
ch.r: The occupational layer that *builds* the AI displacing other roles. Demand is growing 25%+ YoY; pay premiums are widening. The Indian AI-talent paradox in one tile.

ch.src: TeamLease via Reuters Oct 2025; LimeChat case
ch.r: The canonical India-specific AI substitution case. LimeChat: 1 bot at ₹1L/mo replaces ~15 agents. Net BPM hiring down 10× since FY22. 700K jobs at direct, near-term risk.
Document AI (Textract, Document Intelligence, Claude vision) reads forms at higher accuracy than human entry clerks with audit trails. Among the most directly substituted task categories.
F&A outsourcing — invoice processing, reconciliation, financial close. Genpact discloses ~330 GenAI solutions in production already. Junior F&A work substantially automated.
Transaction processing is rule-based document workflow — the highest-fit task for AI agents. Major Indian BPMs are repricing these engagements as outcome-based, not FTE-based.
Medical coding maps clinical narrative to ICD/CPT codes — a high-volume document-classification task. AI coders (3M, Optum, AKASA) match human accuracy; Indian medical-coding BPM is at risk.
Payroll processing, benefits admin, compliance filings. AI agents emerging in employee-query handling; payroll calculations already automated. Mid-tier work exposed.
Indian insurance sales is relationship-driven, but lead qualification, policy comparison, and back-office processing are increasingly AI-driven. PolicyBazaar-style platforms shrink the agent layer.
Branch-level transactional banking is the exact task category UPI + AI customer service replaced. Indian PSU banks shrinking branch headcount; 1.8M jobs structurally exposed.
Bookkeeping, GST filings, tax compliance. AI tools handle 60-70% of routine accounting; CA-level judgement, advisory, and audit work remain. Junior accountants most exposed.
Robo-advisory platforms (Zerodha, Groww, Smallcase) shrink the routine advisory layer. Higher-touch private-wealth and behavioural advisory remain. Mixed exposure.
Loan underwriting and processing — Indian fintechs (KreditBee, Lendingkart, Paytm) underwrite consumer loans at machine speed. Bank loan officers competing with algorithmic underwriting.
Operations at Indian fintech firms — KYC, fraud review, dispute handling. AI agents handle the majority of L1 queries; ops teams pivoting to exception handling.

Chartered Accountants — audit, tax advisory, M&A. Higher-end CA work (statutory audit, complex tax, deal advisory) requires professional judgment less exposed to AI. Junior CA articleship work substantially exposed.
Branch oversight, local relationship management, compliance supervision. AI assists with dashboards and risk scoring; the people-management and local-trust elements remain human.
Equity research, financial modelling, investment memos. AI tools generate first-draft models and research instantly; senior analysts increasingly review AI output rather than build from scratch.
Order execution and routine advice are platform-mediated; differentiated brokerages move to algorithmic execution and quantitative research. Retail broking commoditised.
In-person K-12 teaching at government schools. AI tutoring tools augment but cannot substitute classroom management, social-emotional development, and the credentialing function of teachers.
Government clerical work — file movement, data entry, certificate processing — is highly automatable. Politically protected in India: task exposure high, employment outcome insulated.
Field policing, investigation, community management. AI assists with surveillance, evidence analysis, and FIR drafting; the core field work is irreducible.
Mix of clerical, technical, and field roles across PSUs. Task-level exposure varies widely; employment is heavily protected. Score reflects task-mix, not employment risk.
Government doctors, ANMs, ASHAs. India has acute healthcare-worker shortage; demand far exceeds supply. AI assists diagnosis but expands access rather than substitutes labour.
Military personnel. AI reshapes intelligence, surveillance, and weapons systems but the field-soldier role remains. Specialised technical roles see higher AI augmentation.
Indian Railways staff — mix of station ops, train ops, maintenance, clerical. Ticketing largely automated; scheduling and predictive maintenance are AI-augmented; track and signal staff irreducible.
Postal sorting and delivery. Sorting is already heavily automated; last-mile delivery handled by India Post against a shrinking letter-volume backdrop. Office-side roles most exposed.
Hands-on patient care, medication administration, physical assessment. AI assists documentation and triage but cannot substitute the bedside role. India has a structural nursing shortage.
Diagnostic AI (radiology, dermatology, pathology) assists but doctors retain treatment decisions and patient relationships. Specialist work less exposed; general OPD work more so.
Indian retail pharmacy is largely transactional dispensing — exposed to online pharmacy platforms (Tata 1mg, PharmEasy). Clinical pharmacist roles less exposed.
Hospital admin — billing, insurance coordination, scheduling, supply management. Document AI and scheduling agents directly substitute most of this work.
Sample handling remains physical; result analysis and image interpretation are AI-augmented. Pathology and radiology technicians see higher exposure than histology lab staff.
Radiology AI is among the most mature medical AI domains. India has acute radiologist shortage so AI initially expands access; long-term, demand growth slows as productivity rises 3-5×.
Private K-12 teaching. AI tutoring tools (Khan Academy, BYJU's, ChatGPT) augment learning but the classroom credentialing function holds.
1-on-1 supplementary tutoring — direct competition from AI tutors at near-zero marginal cost. Top-end tutoring (board exams, IIT-JEE) more defensible than primary-school tuition.
Higher-ed teaching, research, and administration. Research work AI-augmented; teaching credentialing and mentorship remain human. Lab-based fields less exposed.
Test-prep coaching (JEE, NEET, UPSC, CAT). ChatGPT-class models solve the majority of objective problems and explain solutions. Test-prep business model under pressure.
Edtech operations roles — content production, ops, sales. Content authoring increasingly AI-driven; sales remain human. Sector itself contracting since Byju's collapse.
Creator economy. AI tools augment content production but the audience attachment is irreducibly human (parasocial). New AI-generated creators emerging but real-creator demand growing.
Among the most directly substituted occupations by current AI. SEO blog content, marketing collateral, product descriptions are GPT-4-class quality at near-zero marginal cost.
Midjourney, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion already produce design output competitive with mid-level human designers. Brand-specific and senior creative work less exposed.
Content calendar planning, copy generation, performance reporting. AI agents handle drafting and scheduling; senior strategy and brand voice remain human.
Event and product photography remain physical; stock photography substantially substituted by generated images. Wedding/event most defensible; commercial stock photography most exposed.
Video editing pipelines have AI features (auto-cut, captioning, b-roll generation) integrated. Long-form storytelling editors less exposed than short-form social-media editors.
VFX pipelines are heavily AI-augmented (rotoscoping, in-betweening, upscaling). Indian VFX shops adopting AI tools to compete on cost; junior roles most affected.
Neural machine translation achieved near-human quality 2-3 years ago. Per-word rates have collapsed 60-80%. Surviving demand is specialised post-editing only.
News aggregation, summarisation, and basic reporting are heavily AI-augmented. Investigative and feature journalism more defensible. Indian news budgets shrinking independent of AI.
Litigation — in-court advocacy, client management, strategic judgment — remains human. Brief preparation, research, and document drafting are AI-augmented (~50% productivity uplift in research).
Recruitment screening, employee-query handling, policy compliance — substantial AI substitution. HRBP and senior org-design roles less exposed.
Document review, legal research, contract redlining — exactly what current LLMs do at or near attorney quality. Indian legal-process-outsourcing severely exposed.
Contract review, compliance, due-diligence — increasingly AI-assisted. Indian in-house counsel reshaping toward higher-judgement, lower-volume work.
Management consulting — research, modelling, deck production are heavily AI-augmented. Client relationships and senior judgement remain. Mid-tier consultants most exposed.
Long-haul driving. Autonomous trucking is far from deployment in Indian conditions (road quality, traffic patterns, regulatory environment). 5-10 year horizon at minimum.
App-platform driving (Ola, Uber, Rapido). Autonomous vehicles 10+ years out in India. Dispatch and pricing AI optimises but does not substitute the driver.
Last-mile delivery — Zomato, Swiggy, Zepto, Blinkit, Amazon, Flipkart. Quick-commerce growth is creating demand far faster than AI substitutes; one of the fastest-growing Indian job categories.
Warehouse operations. Large e-commerce warehouses (Amazon, Flipkart) deploying robotics + AI vision; SME warehouses still manual. Mixed exposure.
Logistics coordination — load planning, route optimisation, freight matching, customs documentation. Heavy document workflow + agentic optimisation. Direct substitution risk.
Commercial aviation. AI assists flight management, weather routing, and predictive maintenance; regulatory and trust factors keep the pilot role for 15+ years.
Service, kitchen, and front-of-house. Ordering substituted by tablets/apps; service and cooking remain physical. Cloud-kitchens automating prep but the labour pool remains large.
Front-desk substantially self-service; housekeeping and F&B service remain. AI assists revenue management and guest queries; physical hospitality holds.
Vendor coordination, logistics, client management. AI assists planning tools and supplier matching; on-the-day execution remains human-intensive.
AI travel companions (apps with multimodal LLMs, AR overlays) substitute the information role; in-person hosting and storytelling remain. Higher-end guided experiences most defensible.
Online travel platforms (MakeMyTrip, Goibibo, EaseMyTrip) plus AI trip-planning have substantially eliminated routine travel agency. Specialist travel (luxury, niche) survives.
